rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE contract content, with type scope for assumptions/guarantees and implementation scope for assignments.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE expressions use only visible identifiers and maintain type consistency.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the model's configured dependencies.
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs, avoiding implementation-only subcomponent paths in type annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax uses assume/guarantee, eq, const, and assign statements within annex agree blocks.
Helper functions and variables must be declared in the same annex and match the expected types (int, real, bool).
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or using subcomponent ports in type contracts.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE variables
Unresolved identifier or type issues in AGREE expressions
Boolean data range comparisons with opaque types
